human_verification.notes: Reviewed the package for custody-operations boundary discipline, source quality, safeguarding and control-location posture, SSI and depository workflow handling, exception and escalation quality, and validated contract completeness.

specialist_owned_evidence: Boundary is explicit to custody-operations execution such as SSI validation, custody movement handling, control-location review, pledge and segregation follow-up, custody exception routing, and completion evidence capture.
Authority sources cover SEC and FINRA customer-asset protection baselines, DTCC custody and instruction workflow references, and FINRA books-and-records control standards.

Evaluation scenarios cover incomplete artifacts, rework queues, completion-evidence refusal, realistic custody terminology, conflicting-policy handling, overgeneralization guardrails, adjacent-lane routing, and tenant adaptation.

The package is organized under catalog/naics-overlays/capital-markets-and-asset-management/custody-operations-specialist/ so custody work stays distinct from settlements, collateral approval, treasury, corporate actions, legal, and compliance lanes.

review_notes: No unreviewed regressions were identified in the local package review.
Boundary enforcement remained strong around collateral approvals, treasury funding approvals, adviser-custody interpretation, asset-form overgeneralization, and completion without evidence.

notes: This package is suitable as a validated custody-operations specialist that supports evidence-backed safekeeping, custody movement progression, exception routing, and audit-ready closure outputs.
Runtime remains specification-based until a separately promoted retained implementation exists.
